name: Fusion Energy Q Factor
description: Best achieved ratio of fusion energy output to energy input (Q factor). Tracks progress from early tokamaks through NIF ignition and ITER projections. Q > 1 means net energy gain.

data_points.source: Alcator C tokamak at MIT — early confinement experiments achieving very low Q
JET (Joint European Torus) — first controlled DT fusion, Q~0.12
TFTR (Princeton) — achieved Q=0.27 with DT fuel in 1994
JET — record 16.1 MW fusion power, Q=0.67 with DT fuel
NIF — August 2021 shot achieving 1.35 MJ yield vs 1.9 MJ laser input (Q=0.7 of laser, but ~0.33 wall-plug), setting stage for ignition
NIF — December 5, 2022: first ignition shot. 3.15 MJ yield from 2.05 MJ laser energy. Q=1.54 (laser energy basis)
NIF — subsequent shots in 2023-2024 achieved up to ~1.9 Q (laser basis); multiple repeat ignition shots confirmed
